summary: Agent OPD should optimize trajectories or critical steps, not only final-answer text. Tool observations are environment outputs and must be treated as prompt/context, not trainable model output.

references.title: Reinforced Self-Training (ReST) meets ReAct: Self-Improvement for Multi-Step Reasoning LLM Agent
references.lesson: Collect ReAct trajectories on training questions, score outcomes, train on accepted trajectories and contrast accepted vs rejected trajectories, then iterate with a growing batch.

references.title: Direct Multi-Turn Preference Optimization for Language Agents
references.lesson: Single-turn DPO assumptions break for multi-turn agents; length and state-action occupancy matter for trajectory preferences.

references.title: AgentQ: Advanced Reasoning and Learning for Autonomous AI Agents
references.lesson: Use search/MCTS-style exploration to produce successful and failed trajectories at comparable states, then optimize off-policy preferences.

references.title: Step-DPO: Step-wise Preference Optimization for Long-chain Reasoning of LLMs
references.lesson: Construct pairs as shared prefix plus preferred next step vs rejected next step to localize credit assignment.

references.title: SDPO: Segment-Level Direct Preference Optimization for Social Agents
references.lesson: Apply preference loss only from the divergence segment onward instead of penalizing identical prefixes.

references.title: Search-R1: Training LLMs to Reason and Leverage Search via RL
references.lesson: Mask retrieved/search-result tokens from loss; only model-generated thoughts, tool calls, and answers should receive gradient.

references.title: WebRL: Training LLM Web Agents via Self-Evolving Online Curriculum Reinforcement Learning
references.lesson: Web/search agents benefit from online rollout, outcome reward models, and curriculum over static offline answer pairs.

references.title: FireAct: Toward Language Agent Fine-tuning
references.lesson: Tool-use fine-tuning needs diverse full trajectories; format-following and tool reasoning must be trained explicitly.

references.title: Agent-FLAN: Designing Data and Methods of Effective Agent Tuning for Large Language Models
references.lesson: Separate format learning, capability learning, and hallucination mitigation; mixing them can cause tool-use side effects.

implementation_decisions: Use LlamaFactory action export for step-level DPO: prompt is the shared prefix including prior observations, chosen/rejected are divergent next tool calls or final-answer calls.
Keep observations in the prompt so PairwiseDatasetProcessor masks them with IGNORE_INDEX; do not put observations in chosen/rejected responses.
Use long cutoff_len for agent trajectories: 32768 for current 2Wiki/BrowseComp work, with 65536 as the next option if BrowseComp traces exceed the context.
Filter to same-question outcome-different pairs and require winner correctness; drop both-wrong pairs and wrong-over-right labels unless inverted by gold correctness.
Continue to use one adapter for all benchmarks, but compare it under the same harness and held-out ids for each benchmark.
